ops.title: semble[mcp]>=0.5 floor for index freshness and API compatibility
ops.body:
# semble[mcp]>=0.5 floor — why it exists and what not to change

`Semble.MCPSpec` (internal/backend/semble.go) launches `uvx --from 'semble[mcp]>=0.5' semble` — exactly this shape. Two independent constraints pin it:

## The `>=0.5` floor is a freshness guarantee
- semble **0.5.0** (PyPI, 2026-07-08) revalidates local-path indexes on **every MCP query**: `_IndexCache.get()` → `_evict_if_stale()` runs the mtime/file-set check (`get_validated_cache`) with a cooldown of 3× the last build duration (`_MIN_REVALIDATE_FACTOR = 3`), then evicts + rebuilds. Staleness is bounded to seconds.
- semble **<0.5** caches each repo **once per MCP-server-process lifetime** (no watcher unless a launch-time path was given, which ccx never passed) — semantic search served session-stale results after any edit. Dropping the floor reintroduces that bug on machines with an older cached/installed resolution.

## No positional path may ride along
- 0.5.0 **removed** the 0.4.x pre-index/watcher positional (`serve(path, ref)` + watchfiles `start_watcher`). Its `_mcp_main` parser accepts only `--content`/`--include-text-files`; any positional → `semble: error: unrecognized arguments: …` → the child dies on the MCP initialize handshake (`connect semble: … initialize: EOF`), and every retry respawns a fresh failing child.
- This was learned the hard way: a fix that appended `os.Getwd()` (designed against the locally installed 0.4.0 source — version-skew trap: `uv tool list` semble ≠ what `uvx --from` resolves) broke every semble MCP launch until reverted.

## Also standing (pre-existing, still true)
- Do NOT unify MCPSpec with the CLI `resolve()`: a bare on-PATH `semble` has no MCP-server mode and exits on the initialize handshake. The uvx `[mcp]` extra is what provides it.
- No upper version bound by policy: floating latest is deliberate (a ceiling would have blocked the 0.5.0 fix itself).
- `TestSembleMCPSpec` (internal/backend/backend_test.go) guards the argv across all three resolution cases; it asserts shape only — it never launches semble, so a live handshake check is still worth doing when touching this.
ops.when: before modifying semble[mcp] version or MCP launch arguments
